title: Emerging Technologies
description: RAND played a pivotal role in emerging technologies throughout the 20th century, from satellites and computers to information-sharing systems, packet switching, and artificial intelligence. RAND now explores the policy implications of emerging technologies across a broad scope of areas such as patient outcomes and health care quality; energy and environmental standards; privacy; military weapon systems; and training teachers and students in the 21st century.
text:
The Geopolitics of AGI: How Might Advanced AI Reshape Global Politics and Security?
How might advanced AI, including the potential emergence of artificial general intelligence (AGI), reshape global politics and security? Jim Mitre and Joel Predd discuss RAND's Geopolitics of AGI initiative and explore what policymakers need to know.
Developing a Risk-Scoring Tool for Artificial Intelligence–Enabled Biological Design: A Method to Assess the Risks of Using Artificial Intelligence to Modify Select Viral Capabilities
A rise in artificial intelligence (AI) use in biology has driven transformative developments but could also pose dual-use risks. This report presents a multifaceted scoring system to assess the risks of using AI to modify select viral capabilities.
Measuring Biological Capabilities and Risks of AI Agents: Generating and Interpreting Evidence from Agentic Evaluations
This perspective examines biological agentic evaluations as a tool for assessing risks from increasingly autonomous AI systems and offers practical guidance on how evaluation design shapes conclusions about biological capability and risk.
Bridging the Digital to Physical Divide: Evaluating LLM Agents on Benchtop DNA Acquisition
This report describes an automatable evaluation of eight frontier large language model (LLM) agents on their ability to design DNA segments, interact with a benchtop DNA synthesizer, and generate laboratory protocols.
A Networked Leapfrog Strategy to Recapture Technology Leadership
Leapfrogging current-generation technologies to create entirely new paradigms may allow the United States to regain leadership in scientific and technological progress that is one of the foremost elements of the U.S.-China rivalry.
Tech Can Avert Catastrophic Fires. What’s Missing Is Coordination
New technologies could prevent routine fires from turning into disasters. But the United States lacks a coherent system for advancing and scaling these innovations.
Are AI Companies Actually Ready to Play God?
As chatbots evolve into a daily tool for advice, emotional support, and spiritual guidance, how can we ensure that artificial intelligence truly benefits those who turn to it in moments of need?
Would You Let Claude Do Your Taxes?
Today's large language models can write code and polish prose. But until people can trust AI to do their taxes, they shouldn't trust it to do their jobs. That gives us a little time to prepare—but not much.
The Dynamics Behind Artificial Intelligence's Impact on Productivity Growth
The author provides a primer on what productivity is, why it is difficult to measure, what historical patterns reveal, and how these insights apply to evaluating the effect of artificial intelligence on the U.S. economy.
